understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete each level by moving the selectable piece(s) so that every goal tile marked '+' in the semantic grid is occupied by a selectable simultaneously; when all goals are covered at once the level reports WIN, and winning the final level completes the run.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each turn controls the currently selected piece shown as 'P' (with other controllable pieces as 'S'). MOVE actions shift the selected piece by one grid cell (3 pixels) in the chosen direction unless blocked by walls/bounds or other objects; if a pushable block is immediately adjacent in the move direction, the move can push it along as long as it has space. Walls '#' act as immovable obstacles, though some boundaries can behave inconsistently so trying moves is sometimes necessary. CLICK switches control to another selectable by providing its exact pixel position from the state (same coordinate system as player.position/objects), letting you reposition multiple pieces to cover all '+' tiles simultaneously within the step budget.

history.summary: Turn 1: MOVE_RIGHT | All directions show adjacent walls; probe for any non-blocking interaction by attempting to move right toward the interior gap if any.
history.reasoning: All directions show adjacent walls; probe for any non-blocking interaction by attempting to move right toward the interior gap if any.

history.summary: Turn 2: CLICK | We appear boxed in by walls; try switching to the other selectable to see if it has a movable escape route.
history.reasoning: We appear boxed in by walls; try switching to the other selectable to see if it has a movable escape route.

history.summary: Turn 3: MOVE_RIGHT | Although marked blocked by walls on all sides, this piece may still be able to slide/push due to boundary quirks; try moving right to test if the interior wall allows movement.
history.reasoning: Although marked blocked by walls on all sides, this piece may still be able to slide/push due to boundary quirks; try moving right to test if the interior wall allows movement.

history.summary: Turn 4: CLICK | Current selectable is boxed in by walls; switch to the other selectable which may have movement options to reach the goal area.
history.reasoning: Current selectable is boxed in by walls; switch to the other selectable which may have movement options to reach the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 5: CLICK | We’re enclosed by walls; the only way to progress is to switch to the other selectable and probe for any movable interaction/opening near it.
history.reasoning: We’re enclosed by walls; the only way to progress is to switch to the other selectable and probe for any movable interaction/opening near it.

history.summary: Turn 6: CLICK | We’re boxed in, so switch to the other selectable and probe for an opening toward the goal area.
history.reasoning: We’re boxed in, so switch to the other selectable and probe for an opening toward the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 7: MOVE_RIGHT | We appear enclosed by walls, but the hint may be unreliable; try moving right to test for any pass-through/teleport behavior at the boundary.
history.reasoning: We appear enclosed by walls, but the hint may be unreliable; try moving right to test for any pass-through/teleport behavior at the boundary.

history.summary: Turn 8: MOVE_UP | We’re enclosed by walls on all four sides, so try moving into a wall to see if it acts like a wrap/teleport or sliding boundary.
history.reasoning: We’re enclosed by walls on all four sides, so try moving into a wall to see if it acts like a wrap/teleport or sliding boundary.

history.summary: Turn 9: MOVE_RIGHT | We appear enclosed but should still probe for a pass-through wall; try moving right against the adjacent wall to test for a shift/teleport/slide behavior.
history.reasoning: We appear enclosed but should still probe for a pass-through wall; try moving right against the adjacent wall to test for a shift/teleport/slide behavior.

history.summary: Turn 10: MOVE_RIGHT | We’re boxed in by walls on all sides; try moving right to test if the adjacent selectable can be pushed or if any boundary allows passage.
history.reasoning: We’re boxed in by walls on all sides; try moving right to test if the adjacent selectable can be pushed or if any boundary allows passage.

history.summary: Turn 11: MOVE_RIGHT | All directions are currently wall-adjacent; try moving into the wall to see if there is a wrap/teleport/slide behavior.
history.reasoning: All directions are currently wall-adjacent; try moving into the wall to see if there is a wrap/teleport/slide behavior.

history.summary: Turn 12: CLICK | We’re boxed in by walls; switch to the other selectable which likely has access to the goal region.
history.reasoning: We’re boxed in by walls; switch to the other selectable which likely has access to the goal region.

history.summary: Turn 13: MOVE_RIGHT | Move onto the right-side goal tile to occupy it with this selectable.
history.reasoning: Move onto the right-side goal tile to occupy it with this selectable.

history.summary: Turn 14: MOVE_LEFT | We’re adjacent to the goal area on the left; move left to step onto the + region.
history.reasoning: We’re adjacent to the goal area on the left; move left to step onto the + region.

history.summary: Turn 15: MOVE_UP | We need the selected piece to occupy the goal region (+); moving up should place it onto the central goal tile area.
history.reasoning: We need the selected piece to occupy the goal region (+); moving up should place it onto the central goal tile area.
